headline: Bloom Energy (BE) Valuation Check As Strong Q1 2026 Results And Oracle AI Deal Lift Expectations
summary: Bloom Energy (BE) has jumped into focus after a busy late April, with Q1 2026 earnings beating analyst expectations, full year guidance raised to US$3.4b to US$3.8b, and an expanded Oracle partnership for AI data center power. See our latest analysis for Bloom Energy. headline: After Blowout Quarter, This Is AMD’s Price Prediction For 2026
summary: Our 24/7 Wall St. price target for Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) is $398.04, which sits modestly below where shares currently trade after a blistering rally. With AMD changing hands at $416.36 and the model implying roughly -4.4% from here, the recommendation is hold with 90% confidence. Near fair value is the cleanest way to describe ... headline: Banks Rework Oracle Data Center Debt As AI And Green Power Grow
summary: Banks including JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, SMBC, MUFG, and Bank of America are working to distribute and de risk large data center loans tied to Oracle projects. The loans are linked to Oracle's US data center buildout, including Michigan and New Mexico campuses under Project Jupiter. Lenders are using private loan sales and discounted risk transfers as capital needs for AI infrastructure grow. Project Jupiter includes a move toward clean onsite power, signaling a shift in how hyperscale AI...

headline: North American AI Data Center Expansion Drives 2026 CapEx of Top Nine CSPs to US$830 Billion, Says TrendForce
summary: TrendForce's latest findings on the AI industry highlight that several major North American CSPs have recently raised their 2026 capital expenditure (CapEx) guidance in response to strong AI demand. As a result, TrendForce has revised its forecast for the combined CapEx of the world's top nine CSPs—Google, AWS, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, ByteDance, Tencent, Alibaba, and Baidu—up to approximately US$830 billion in 2026, with the annual growth rate raised from 61% to 79%.
